GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "H&M is transforming Australia's online shopping landscape for the better", "Fashion retail in Australia has changed dramatically in the last decade. And while some companies have failed to adapt with the times, H&M has barely missed a beat. When its customers asked for a more sustainable offering, the trendsetting retailer listened. The launch of its first dedicated online store in Australia is a testament to this. H&M's e-commerce platform isn't just a premium shopping experience — it's an education. Each garment includes a detailed description that outlines its country of origin and production, its material composition, as well as tips on how to wash and care for the item in the most environmentally-friendly way. Of course, it's one thing to know you're purchasing with purpose. But H&M's collections, which capture the fashion zeitgeist every time, empower us to look and feel good when putting an outfit together. Recently, Vogue editor Rebecca Bonavia styled three such outfits, from H&M's new season range. Included in her edit are pieces from the Spring Conscious Collection, such as an ethereal maxi dress crafted from 100 per cent recycled fabric. "It's something that everyone is now looking for," says Rebecca Bonavia of the cultural shift in fashion, which has seen brands up-cycle existing materials into new styles. Another of Vogue's key looks is a shorts and blazer ensemble that marries pastel tones with more earthy shades. "If shorts aren't your thing, you can definitely style this look with a skirt or pants," says Rebecca Bonavia. Each of these looks can be purchased from H&M's Australian-exclusive online store, alongside the brand's wildly popular in-house Fashion Studio Collections and their infamous designer collaboration collections, which have previously involved collaborations with of-the-moment luxury designers such as Stella McCartney, Maison Martin Margiela and Versace. SHOP THE LOOKS"
